name: Vernonia amygdalina
mechanism: insulin-sensitization and inhibition of tumor proliferation
interactions: Enhances effect of antidiabetic and chemotherapeutic drugs
safetyNotes: Moderate risk: side effects or medication interactions are plausible. Avoid use during pregnancy unless advised by a clinician; Lower dose or stop if nausea, vomiting, or GI upset occurs.
description: Widely used in West Africa for malaria, diabetes, and cancer adjunct therapy
dosage: Leaf juice or infusion, 100–200 ml daily
preparation: Crushed fresh leaves or boiled infusion
